tensions.actual: test actual
tensions.desired: test desired

tensions.actual: Conceptual foundation exists and is partially implemented. Data model: gestures, sessions, epochs tables; gesture_id + actual_at on mutations; parent_snapshot_json on child creation. Temporal calculus: implied execution window, urgency, sequencing pressure, critical path (recursive), containment violation — all computed in sd-core. Frontier projection: operating envelope partitions children into next/overdue/held/remaining/resolved. Three interface surfaces operational: TUI (functional but pre-foundation), CLI (30+ commands with foundation vocabulary, JSON on all commands), MCP (werk mcp — 30 tools via rmcp/stdio, same gestures as CLI). Cooperative dynamics remain unexplored.
tensions.desired: werk is a mature tool for practicing structural dynamics — individually and cooperatively

tensions.actual: TUI rendering layer rebuilt on ftui (Panel, Paragraph, StatusLine, TextInput). Gaze card is the visual anchor. Grab-and-drop reordering, temporal indicators, alert system, edit mode, search/move overlays, agent prompt, insight review all working. Unicode width issues in selected state highlight remain. Three independent design systems produced by Claude, Gemini, and Codex agents — each explored ftui and werk from scratch and wrote a comprehensive design system document plus reflection on gaps and decision hierarchy. All collected in werk-design-inputs/ for synthesis. Next: synthesize the three designs into a definitive design system, then implement.
tensions.desired: werk is a FrankenTUI-first application where the terminal interface is the primary experience

tensions.actual: Fritz's framework addresses collective structure hierarchically; a git-snapshot cooperation model is articulated (export JSON, fork/merge via PRs, owner reviews diffs) but not yet implemented; the peer-to-peer evolutionary model remains a research question
tensions.desired: a clear structural model exists for how multiple participants hold and evolve shared tension fields

tensions.actual: course is scheduled for end of April 2026; no direct input yet from the structural consulting methodology on cooperative dynamics
tensions.desired: the Foundations for Structural Thinking course has been completed and its insights on collective structure are integrated into the cooperative model

tensions.actual: werk flush command writes tensions.json to workspace root — a git-trackable JSON file containing all tensions as raw structural data; .werk/ remains gitignored; consumers can reconstruct the tree and compute dynamics from the flushed data without the CLI
tensions.desired: workspace state is visible in git diffs and PRs without requiring the CLI

tensions.actual: Note commands restructured as noun-verb subcommands (note add/rm/list). Retraction via note_retracted mutation. All tests pass. Ready to resolve.
tensions.desired: Note deletion capability: delete or soft-delete notes that were added to the wrong tension

tensions.actual: Interactive testing complete. Gaze card, help screen, grab-and-drop reordering, temporal alignment, alert truncation, empty descended state, rules width all verified and working. Unicode display width vs char count issue in selected tension background highlight remains. Three-agent design system generation process completed — independent design documents and reflections collected from Claude, Gemini, and Codex for synthesis into definitive visual coherence plan.
tensions.desired: rendering rebuild is visually verified and interaction-tested

tensions.actual: test
tensions.desired: test

tensions.actual: CLI has foundation vocabulary (survey, ground, horizon, note testimony, hold/position, compose, epoch). JSON output on all commands. Context command provides structural JSON (context --all, --urgent, <id>) with urgency, staleness, frontier. Batch applies YAML mutations grouped as one gesture. run/watch deleted. Agent orchestration vocabulary purged. `werk mcp` subcommand starts stdio MCP server — the protocol surface. Remaining gaps: help output is flat (30+ commands alphabetically), no grouped help by framework.
tensions.desired: CLI is a complete, honest interface to the structure — foundation vocabulary, readable output, structured JSON, every operative gesture available, no agent-specific orchestration commands

tensions.actual: CLI human-facing output fully aligned with foundation vocabulary: Reality (not Actual), Deadline (not Horizon) in show/add/compose-up/horizon commands. CLI aliases: werk actual → reality, werk deadline → horizon. --phase filter removed from list. 'phase transition' → 'epoch boundary'. Agent prompts updated. DynamicsEngine renamed to Engine. dynamics.rs consolidated into temporal.rs (sd-core), renamed to serialize.rs (CLI). JSON keys and struct fields unchanged (backward compatible). Remaining: TUI labels (blocked on TUI rebuild).
tensions.desired: consistent terminology across CLI, TUI, and agent-facing documentation — unambiguous names for the werkspace, its contents, and the operations on them

tensions.actual: Three design system documents (33KB Claude, 26KB Codex, 9KB Gemini) and three reflection documents collected in werk-design-inputs/. Two prior design docs also available. No synthesis started. Agent flywheel methodology (agent-flywheel.com) identified as process reference.
tensions.desired: the three independent design systems are synthesized into a single definitive design system that becomes the implementation blueprint

tensions.actual: Substantial implementation of the conceptual foundation in the data model and CLI. Implemented: gesture grouping (all mutations linked to gestures), sessions and epochs tables with CRUD, temporal calculus (6 computed properties in temporal.rs), frontier projection (operating envelope in frontier.rs), held/positioned distinction, parent snapshots, epoch boundaries on reality/desire updates. CLI displays frontier facts with honest labels and sorts children by structural position. Not yet implemented: state machine (6 states identified conceptually, not formally specified — #16), delta versioning as a queryable sequence of structural snapshots, epoch/session lifecycle wiring (#36). The gap between 'data model supports X' and 'the instrument uses X as the foundation specifies' remains significant — tables exist but the behavioral layer (state machine governing transitions, thresholds, pathway palettes) is unbuilt.
tensions.desired: the conceptual foundation is implemented — data model supports delta versioning, gesture grouping, order of operations distinct from horizon, held steps, and the five temporal concepts

tensions.actual: All speculative dynamics gutted. sd-core computes only honest facts: urgency (elapsed fraction of horizon), horizon drift (pattern of horizon changes), gap_magnitude (binary — has gap or not), temporal signals (implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path, containment violation), closure progress. Net -15,095 lines across 28 files. 881 tests pass. DynamicsEngine simplified to Store wrapper with parent snapshot capture. CLI JSON output drops dynamics field — shows temporal signals, urgency, overdue, closure instead. strsim dependency removed. TUI updated to render without speculative dynamics. Consider renaming sd-core to werk-core as optional follow-up.
tensions.desired: old dynamics code removed from sd-core — text-similarity magnitude, text-convergence resolution, oscillation, conflict, orientation, creative cycle phase, assimilation depth, compensating strategy, structural tendency all deleted. sd-core computes honest facts and signals only: urgency, temporal properties (implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path, containment), closure progress. No speculative dynamics remain. Consider renaming sd-core to werk-core.

tensions.actual: V1+V2 implemented. Deck view: 4-zone layout (desire/route/console/reality), frontier computation classifying children, gradual compression (route/held/accumulated compress progressively), zero-padded IDs in aligned sub-columns, pitch navigation (j/k), roll descent (l). Performance: DB indexes added, N+1 queries eliminated, render-path queries cached. 28 design questions captured in shaping doc. Next: accumulated items gravity toward reality (Q24), V3 roll navigation polish, V4 gestures.
tensions.desired: TUI rebuilt around the operating envelope as primary interaction surface — frontier-centered screen layout, pitch/roll/yaw navigation, threshold mechanics, descended/horizon view toggle, peek gestures, ground mode access

tensions.actual: state machine states identified conceptually (NORMAL, INPUT, FOCUSED, ORIENTED, PATHWAY, THRESHOLD) but not formally specified
tensions.desired: complete state machine specification — all states, transitions, key bindings per state, designed in XState/Stately before Rust implementation

tensions.actual: Calculus of time fully consolidated in temporal.rs: urgency, horizon drift, gap magnitude moved from dynamics.rs; implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path (recursive), containment violation already there. dynamics.rs deleted. DynamicsEngine renamed to Engine. CLI dynamics.rs renamed to serialize.rs. All imports updated. 859 tests pass.
tensions.desired: calculus of time implemented — implied execution window, urgency from deadline windows, sequencing pressure, critical path (recursive), containment violation detection

tensions.actual: survey view conceptually defined in foundation document but no concrete layout, no implementation
tensions.desired: survey view designed and implemented — temporal framing, structural annotations, frame widening/narrowing, context-carrying yaw transition

tensions.actual: thresholds conceptually defined. No implementation. Hold-key detection feasibility untested across terminals
tensions.desired: threshold mechanics implemented — tap/hold navigation, lingering space visual treatment, screen boundary signals

tensions.actual: Schema fully extended: 3 new tables (gestures, sessions, epochs), 3 new columns (gesture_id + actual_at on mutations, parent_snapshot_json on tensions). Gesture context wired into Store with process-scoped session model. All 11 CLI mutation commands + batch create gestures. Mutation replay tolerates note/position/deleted. CLI: werk hold, werk position, werk resolve --actual-at. Parent snapshots auto-populated as JSON on child creation. Foundation doc updated (sessions, gesture data model, actual_at). Remaining: epoch trigger path, session TUI lifecycle.
tensions.desired: data model extended — gesture grouping (gesture table), run/session tracking (run table), epoch markers, notes as first-class mutations, held vs positioned as explicit state

tensions.actual: CLI has 30+ commands. hold, position, compose-up, epoch, survey, and ground commands added. Old terminology cleaned: 'phase distribution' removed from health description, --phase filter removed from list, 'phase transition' → 'epoch boundary' in reality/desire output, 'actual state' → 'current reality' in help text, agent prompts updated. 'run' command still refers to agent invocation, not sessions (by design — sessions are TUI-scoped). Sort options updated to urgency/name/deadline.
tensions.desired: CLI commands aligned with foundation vocabulary — compose-up, hold/position, ground, survey, epoch marking, run tracking commands added; old commands updated to use new terminology

tensions.actual: All speculative dynamics removed from sd-core. dynamics.rs gutted from 9401 to 686 lines — keeps only Urgency, HorizonDrift, gap_magnitude (binary). engine.rs gutted from 2266 to 277 lines — keeps Store wrapper with parent snapshot capture, no dynamics computation. events.rs gutted from ~1050 to 507 lines — keeps state change events, removes 11 dynamics transition event types (ConflictDetected, LifecycleTransition, OscillationDetected, etc.). lib.rs exports cleaned (99 to 43 lines). strsim dependency removed. CLI dynamics.rs adapter gutted — removed DynamicsJson and all speculative types, keeps only TensionInfo/MutationInfo helpers. show/context/run/health/insights/watch commands updated to use honest facts only. JSON output drops dynamics field entirely. 881 tests pass, net -15,095 lines.
tensions.desired: old dynamics module gutted — all speculative dynamics (magnitude, resolution, oscillation, conflict, orientation, creative cycle phase, assimilation depth, compensating strategy, structural tendency) removed from sd-core. Only honest temporal facts and signals remain. DynamicsEngine simplified or removed. CLI dynamics.rs adapter cleaned up. JSON output updated to drop dynamics fields.

tensions.actual: README install section fixed — removed fictional feature flags. CLI design gaps #53-#56 recorded under #10, two now resolved (#54 reopen --reason, #56 tree subtree). Docs expansion tracked as #52.
tensions.desired: skills and documentation updated to reflect foundation — SKILL.md, references, onboarding rewritten around new vocabulary and concepts

tensions.actual: watch daemon exists with 7 trigger types, pending insights stored in .werk/watch/pending/. No integration with run concept or ground mode. Triggers fire independently of session state
tensions.desired: watch daemon rethought — fits with sessions (session awareness), thresholds (triggers as threshold crossings), and ground mode (insights surface during debrief, not as interruptions)

tensions.actual: No pathway palettes exist in CLI. Gestures that create structural signals (e.g., horizon command setting a child deadline beyond parent) complete silently. No inline response options. Temporal calculus already computes containment violations and sequencing pressure — the detection logic exists, the response options do not.
tensions.desired: pathway palettes in CLI — when a gesture creates a structural signal (containment violation, sequencing pressure, etc.), the instrument immediately presents response options inline. The user selects or dismisses. Synchronous instrument feedback within the gesture flow.

tensions.actual: parent_snapshot_json field exists on tensions. Auto-populated at child creation time in both store.create_tension_full() and engine.create_tension_with_parent(). Captures full JSON with desire, reality, status, horizon, and all children (id, desired, actual, status, position, horizon). parent_desired_snapshot and parent_actual_snapshot still populated for backward compat.
tensions.desired: parent snapshot captures full descended view state — not just desire/reality text but order of operations, held steps, resolved steps, children metadata

tensions.actual: batch command now groups all mutations under one gesture. begin_gesture called before apply loop, end_gesture after. Gesture description includes mutation count.
tensions.desired: batch command aligned with gesture grouping — batch applies a set of mutations as one gesture rather than individual atomic changes

tensions.actual: Dual-layer identity implemented. ULIDs remain canonical machine IDs. Workspace-local short_code (sequential integer, auto-assigned at creation, backfilled on migration) provides human addressing. PrefixResolver accepts both numeric short codes (exact match) and ULID prefixes (4+ chars). Short codes shown in werk show output (#N). Ambiguous prefix errors now show short codes. Zero collision risk for human addressing.
tensions.desired: the identity system is world-class and future-proof — IDs have strong prefix diversity for human addressing, zero collision risk at any resolution, and remain machine-sortable and globally unique

tensions.actual: actual_at field exists on mutations and is persisted. No CLI path to set it. Resolve command does not ask or accept when resolution actually happened.
tensions.desired: actual_at wired into resolve flow — werk resolve accepts --actual-at flag for 'I did this yesterday' cases, configurable prompt option per foundation calculus of time

tensions.actual: CLI trigger path complete: reality and desire commands auto-create epoch boundaries before applying updates. --no-epoch flag for minor corrections. Manual 'werk epoch' command also available. TUI trigger path not yet implemented.
tensions.desired: epoch creation has a trigger path — CLI or TUI prompts 'start a new epoch?' when desire/reality changes, snapshotting the current delta

tensions.actual: Sessions are process-scoped by design. begin_gesture() creates sessionless gestures (CLI, agent). begin_gesture_in_session(session_id) associates with a specific session (TUI). active_session() query still exists for TUI use, but CLI/agent paths never call it. Multiple TUI panes = multiple independent sessions. Agent mutations = sessionless gestures. This is architecturally settled. Remaining: TUI needs to call start_session on open and end_session on close.
tensions.desired: session lifecycle is clear — TUI instances manage their own sessions, CLI commands operate sessionless, agent mutations are sessionless gestures. Multiple concurrent TUI panes are independent sessions.

tensions.actual: Tree view now shows short codes (#N) as primary identity, theory of closure progress [R/T], OVERDUE flags, and status markers (✓/~). Old dynamics (phase badges, movement signals, conflict markers) removed. ULID prefixes no longer shown in tree — short codes are the human interface.
tensions.desired: tree view shows structural position numbering (1., 1.1., 1.1.3.) as orientation chrome alongside short codes — evaluate whether this aids spatial orientation without confusing it with addressing

tensions.actual: werk flush command implemented: writes tensions.json to workspace root with all tensions as raw structural data (id, short_code, desired, actual, status, parent_id, position, horizon, created_at), deterministic output (sorted by short_code, alphabetical keys), pretty-printed for git diff readability; autoflush deferred to v1.1
tensions.desired: tension tree state is flushed to a git-trackable file (JSON/JSONL) in the workspace — manually via a flush command and optionally via autoflush on mutation with debounce (à la beads_rust); git operations remain the users responsibility

tensions.actual: no public-facing surface exists; werk is CLI-only and single-user. werk.horse domain is owned but unused (acquired for a different product). werk.bank is the ideal domain but costs $999
tensions.desired: a public web surface exists where others can view (part of) the tension tree and interact with it — prototype for demonstrating werk and inviting cooperation via Spikes or similar feedback mechanisms

tensions.actual: epoch tables and session tables exist in data model, no CLI trigger paths
tensions.desired: epoch and session lifecycle are fully operational

tensions.actual: business foundation document drafted covering economic thesis, market positioning, revenue architecture, and venture web; no revenue yet; no pricing validated; no public presence beyond statecraft.systems listing
tensions.desired: werk has a sustainable business model that funds continued development while remaining true to the conceptual foundation

tensions.actual: nobody outside immediate circle knows werk exists; no public content, no posts, no demos
tensions.desired: werk is publicly known as the structural intent layer for agentic work — builders in the terminal-native AI ecosystem recognize it

tensions.actual: synthesis articulated in business foundation doc but not yet reflected in licensing, README, or release strategy
tensions.desired: the open-format / proprietary-instrument split is implemented and clearly communicated

tensions.actual: six revenue streams identified and reasoned through; none activated; practitioner license is first candidate
tensions.desired: at least one revenue stream is active and generating income

tensions.actual: integration points mapped in business foundation doc; no implementation; Statecraft website already lists werk as a tool
tensions.desired: werk integrates naturally with Statecraft services, Modern Minuteman operations, and Spikes feedback loops

tensions.actual: post drafted; video script drafted; neither published
tensions.desired: first public post about werk published — X post with Remotion video addressed to Jeffrey Emanuel and the agentic coding community

tensions.actual: concept articulated in business foundation doc; no implementation; no design doc
tensions.desired: coherence offering designed — one-liner or agent prompt that transforms existing workspace into a proposed werk structure

tensions.actual: Complete: reality/desire auto-create epochs, --no-epoch for minor corrections, epoch --show N queries all mutations (tension + descendants) within the epoch span. Ghost geometry builds through normal use and is queryable.
tensions.desired: Reality and desire updates create epoch boundaries — the ghost geometry builds through normal use, mutations are queryable per epoch span

tensions.actual: MCP server implemented. `werk mcp` starts stdio MCP server via rmcp. 30 tools registered across read (show, tree, list, survey, health, ground, diff, context, trajectory, insights), gesture (add, compose, reality, desire, resolve, release, reopen, move, hold, position, horizon, rm, snooze, recur), note (note_add, note_rm, note_list), epoch (epoch, epoch_list, epoch_show), and batch. All tools return structured JSON. Mutation tools trigger autoflush and hook infrastructure. Complex read commands delegate to CLI --json. Tested: initialize handshake, tools/list, show, tree, note_add all verified working.
tensions.desired: werk exposes its operative gestures as an MCP server

tensions.actual: No clipboard integration exists in TUI. User must manually retype or remember tension details when switching to an agent session.
tensions.desired: TUI yank — copy tension snapshots to clipboard for handoff to agent sessions

tensions.actual: Temporal calculus computes properties per-query in temporal.rs but does not monitor or compare across time. Watch daemon has threshold detection coupled to agent invocation — detection logic is valuable, coupling is not.
tensions.desired: threshold detection as independent structural signal logic — urgency crossings, horizon drift, containment violations surfaceable without coupling to any invocation mechanism

tensions.actual: run command deleted (975 lines), watch command deleted (832 lines), run.rs test file deleted, agent-specific cross-area tests removed, which dependency removed. context command kept as general structural query tool. batch command preserved.
tensions.desired: run and watch commands deleted — agent orchestration removed from the instrument

tensions.actual: No staging mechanism exists. Batch command applies YAML mutations immediately. No concept of pending/draft mutations.
tensions.desired: staging mechanism — propose/pending/confirm/reject for asynchronous draft mutations. Multiple independent proposals, each separately confirmable/rejectable. For complex human restructuring and agent-proposed changes held for review.

tensions.actual: Foundation lists 12 operative gestures, all treating tensions as stable identity containers. Split/merge/restructure are performed ad-hoc through desire rewrites and fresh creates, damaging ghost geometry. No provenance field exists. Epochs are per-tension only — cannot represent phase transitions that span tension boundaries. The #25 split into #25+#49 demonstrated the cost: 6 incoherent epochs on #25, amnesiac #49.
tensions.desired: structural transformation gestures (split, merge, restructure) and cross-tension provenance — the foundation and data model support identity-topology-changing operations with lineage tracking

tensions.actual: Hooks exist in code (pre_mutation, post_mutation, post_create, post_resolve) and fire from CLI and MCP gesture tools. No design document. Hook set evolved ad hoc. No pre_create hook (asymmetric). No clarity on whether MCP and CLI should share the same hook events or diverge. Config lives in config.toml — unclear if that is right. No rationale recorded for why these events and not others.
tensions.desired: hook infrastructure has a clear design — rationale for when hooks fire, purpose of each event, firing rules across interfaces (CLI/MCP/TUI), pre/post symmetry, configuration model

tensions.actual: README.md covers the essentials. No docs/ directory, no crate-level reference docs, no user guide, no docs site. Foundation doc exists but is a design document, not user-facing documentation.
tensions.desired: werk has a proper documentation surface — reference docs for each crate, user guide, and foundation for a docs site

tensions.actual: README review surfaced terminology gaps: tree shows the forest (not a subtree), no log/logbook command exists (vocabulary from tui-shaping.md), epoch subcommands use flag style not subcommand style, reading commands (diff/ground/insights/survey/trajectory) have unclear boundaries to new users. Needs systematic terminology pass.
tensions.desired: CLI terminology and command structure reviewed and coherent — tree/forest distinction resolved, log/logbook commands exist, epoch subcommands rationalized, reading commands have clear boundaries

tensions.actual: `reopen --reason` implemented. Records reason as a reopen_reason mutation within the gesture. Both CLI and MCP surfaces support it. Pattern follows release --reason.
tensions.desired: reopen gesture captures a reason — structural record of why something was brought back

tensions.actual: position command accepts single tension + position. No way to set a new order wholesale or partially in one gesture. Batch YAML supports SetPosition but no dedicated reorder command.
tensions.desired: batch positioning exists — reorder multiple tensions in one gesture

tensions.actual: Implemented. `tree <id>` shows the subtree under a specific tension. Works via Forest::subtree(). Both CLI and MCP surfaces support it.
tensions.desired: tree command can show a subtree under a specific tension
